phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: At level 1 your point-click spear bullying beat the wall-frost poke control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Anivia answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Your point-click spear bullying out-muscle Anivia's wall-frost poke control at level 2 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 your point-click spear bullying beat the wall-frost poke control in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Anivia answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Your point-click spear bullying out-muscle Anivia's wall-frost poke control through levels 4-5 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Grand Starfall online — your shoves become bot-lane invoices. The early lead must become the map now.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: First item spike: the stun-spear rotation deletes squishies. Last call before scaling lanes wake.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Pantheon is a starting pistol: R the flank, stun the carry, block the counter — and let the team finish what the spear started.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q the contacts — the spear chunks at one and the empowered version executes; mid rarely respects either.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W point-and-click stun joins: the level 2 all-in is the lane's first ultimatum. Most mids pay it.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: W-stun, empowered Q or E-block — you out-trade everything for five levels. Spend the window loudly.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Empowered-spear the step-ups and block the answer with E — the lane is a toll road with a spear booth.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Grand Starfall online — your shoves become bot-lane invoices. The early lead must become the map now.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: First item spike: the stun-spear rotation deletes squishies. Last call before scaling lanes wake.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Aurelion Sol's stardust ramp control own this window — your point-click spear bullying don't answer them at two-plus items yet. Farm what's safe and let the next spike re-price the lane.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Your point-click spear bullying out-muscle Cassiopeia's grounded-zone DPS at level 1 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: At level 2 your point-click spear bullying beat the grounded-zone DPS in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Cassiopeia answer you, not the other way around.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Your point-click spear bullying out-muscle Cassiopeia's grounded-zone DPS at level 3 — this window is yours, so force the trades and tax every step toward the wave.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Through levels 4-5 the numbers lean your way — point-click spear bullying edge out the grounded-zone DPS when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Slight deficit at the level-6 breakpoint: Cassiopeia's grounded-zone DPS edge out your point-click spear bullying on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: On first item the numbers lean his way — the grounded-zone DPS out-tick your point-click spear bullying in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Pantheon is a starting pistol: R the flank, stun the carry, block the counter — and let the team finish what the spear started.

phases.why: Late Pantheon is a starting pistol: R the flank, stun the carry, block the counter — and let the team finish what the spear started.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q the contacts — the spear chunks at one and the empowered version executes; mid rarely respects either.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W point-and-click stun joins: the level 2 all-in is the lane's first ultimatum. Most mids pay it.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: W-stun, empowered Q or E-block — you out-trade everything for five levels. Spend the window loudly.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Empowered-spear the step-ups and block the answer with E — the lane is a toll road with a spear booth.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Grand Starfall online — your shoves become bot-lane invoices. The early lead must become the map now.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q the contacts — the spear chunks at one and the empowered version executes; mid rarely respects either.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: W point-and-click stun joins: the level 2 all-in is the lane's first ultimatum. Most mids pay it.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: Full kit: W-stun, empowered Q or E-block — you out-trade everything for five levels. Spend the window loudly.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Empowered-spear the step-ups and block the answer with E — the lane is a toll road with a spear booth.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Grand Starfall online — your shoves become bot-lane invoices. The early lead must become the map now.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: First item spike: the stun-spear rotation deletes squishies. Last call before scaling lanes wake.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Pantheon is a starting pistol: R the flank, stun the carry, block the counter — and let the team finish what the spear started.
